description_long: This building is a large, rectangular commercial structure with a prominent overhanging dark-tiled roof and a central entrance flanked by wide staircases. The facade features a mix of smooth white and textured stone surfaces, with the main entrance area highlighted by a sign above the doors and two sets of columns. The side and rear elevations are simpler, with minimal ornamentation and a focus on geometric forms. The roof has a large central skylight or recessed area, possibly for natural lighting. The entrance is approached by a broad, red-tiled staircase, and the overall design is modern with some monumental elements. The building appears to be constructed primarily from concrete, stone, and glass, with metal and wood likely used for doors and window frames.
description: A modern, rectangular watch shop with a dark-tiled overhanging roof, stone and white facade, and a prominent central entrance with wide stairs and signage.
description_view_0: Front view showing the main entrance, wide red-tiled stairs, columns, and a large sign above the doors.
description_view_1: Side view revealing the overhanging roof, stone-textured facade, and minimal windows or ornamentation.
description_view_2: Rear view with a plain white wall, minimal architectural features, and no visible entrances or windows.
description_view_3: Opposite side view similar to view 1, with stone-textured facade and overhanging roof, plus a glimpse of the red-tiled stairs.
attribute_storefront_signage: A large rectangular sign is present above the main entrance, centered on the facade. The sign is white with dark text, displaying 'BẢO TÀNG HÙNG VƯƠNG' and additional smaller text below. The sign is integrated into the building's architecture, with no visible logos or decorative elements beyond the text.
attribute_architectural_style: Contemporary with monumental elements, characterized by a flat, overhanging roof, geometric forms, a mix of smooth and textured surfaces, and a prominent, symmetrical entrance with wide stairs and columns.
